rulings.Case Title: AMY MORAN VS. QWEST COMMUNICATIONS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, December 15, 2009, line 29, DEFENDANTS QWEST COMMUNICATIONS, DENNIS SHERWOOD MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT or in the alternative summary adjudication. Motion for summary judgment denied. Motion for summary adjudication granted in part and denied in part. Summary adjudication granted for first cause of action, discrimination. No evidence that adverse action was taken because of plaintiff's gender. Further, defendant offers a nondiscriminatory reason for the conduct (to bring quota in line with branch expectations) and plaintiff fails to establish this is a mere pretext. Motion denied for second cause of action, harassment. Triable issue of fact whether conduct severe and pervasive. (Moran declaration.) Triable issue of fact whether Sherwood's conduct was such to create a hostile working environment. Motion denied for third cause of action, failure to prevent harassment. Triable issue of fact raised whether defendant's procedure sufficient to address plaintiff's harassment concerns. Motion denied for fourth cause of action, termination in violation of public policy. Triable issue of fact raised whether working conditions were made intolerable due to the treatment she received from Sherwood and Qwest's disregard of her complaint. Summary adjudication granted as to fifth cause of action for failure to make immediate payment of wages upon discharge and waiting time penalties. No triable issue of fact that plaintiff was paid all the compensation she was entitled to under the compensation agreement. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: CARMEN CORTEZ VS. SERVICE EMPLOYEES INTERNATIONAL UNION LOCAL 87 et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Judgment On The Pleadings And Req. For Judicial Ntc
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, December 15, 2009, line 9, DEFENDANT SERVICE EMPLOYEES INTERNATIONAL UNION LOCAL 87 Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings. Granted with thirty days leave to amend as to second, fourth, and fifth caues of action, and as to plaintiff's claim for failure to pay overtime wages in first cause of action. Plaintiff must allege amount of wages that remain unpaid, when plaintiff discharged or quit and when received last paycheck due, to plead specific facts supporting the fourth cause of action, and demonstrate compliance with CCP section 382. Granted without leave to amend as to third cause of action and plaintiff's claim for failure to pay regular wages. =(302/CWW)
